page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c1.large is an x86_64 architecture server instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 8 dedicated physical cores with 8 vCPUs, operating with a 1.0 thread-per-core ratio, meaning hyperthreading is disabled. The instance is configured with 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per CPU core.

This server type does not include any bundled local storage, requiring external block storage volumes for operating system and application data. It lacks GPU hardware accelerators and does not provide complimentary public IPv4 addresses. Additionally, advanced CPU instruction sets, such as Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX, AVX512), Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX), and hardware-assisted cryptography (AES, SHA_NI), are not supported by this hardware profile.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the ecs.c1.large offers a dedicated compute allocation with a 1:2 CPU-to-RAM ratio, positioning it as an entry-level compute-focused resource. Due to the lack of local storage, hardware-accelerated cryptography, and vector extensions, it is designed for workloads that rely on basic integer calculations, lightweight web serving, or distributed compute nodes where advanced instruction sets and local caching are not required.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c1.large is an x86_64 compute-optimized server instance featuring 8 dedicated physical cores and 8 vCPUs with hyperthreading disabled. It provides 16.0 GB of system memory, establishing a 2.0 GB per core ratio. The instance operates on the KVM hypervisor and does not include local storage, requiring network-attached storage for data persistence. There are no integrated GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses. Hardware-level instruction sets, including AVX, AVX512, AMX, and AES, are unavailable on this instance type. This server is structured for basic compute tasks, lightweight web serving, and distributed processing nodes that require dedicated CPU resources without relying on advanced vector instructions or local disk performance.

tagline: A dedicated compute-focused instance featuring eight physical cores, a 1:2 CPU-to-RAM ratio, and network-only storage configurations.
